---
name: frontloom-svelte
description: Use this skill for Svelte 5 and SvelteKit frontend tasks. Enforces idiomatic Svelte patterns including runes ($state, $derived, $effect, $props), snippets, SvelteKit load functions, form actions, typed props, and SvelteKit conventions. Always use alongside frontloom-core-fe for baseline quality standards.
---

# Frontloom Svelte

Apply this skill to all Svelte 5 and SvelteKit work.
Use alongside `frontloom-core-fe` for baseline frontend quality standards.

## Goal

Produce Svelte code that is idiomatic, type-safe, minimal, and production-ready.
Follow Svelte's mental model — less code, less abstraction, more direct.

## Principles

1. Svelte compiles away — write what you mean, the compiler handles the rest.
2. Runes (`$state`, `$derived`, `$effect`) are the reactivity primitives. Use them directly.
3. Components are `.svelte` files. Logic, markup, and styles colocate naturally.
4. SvelteKit handles routing, data loading, and form actions. Lean into it.
5. Less abstraction is better — Svelte's compiler eliminates the need for many patterns other frameworks require.
6. Server-first by default — load data on the server, render on the server, enhance on the client.
7. Progressive enhancement is built into SvelteKit form actions — use it.

---

## Component Patterns

### Basic Component

```svelte
<!-- UserCard.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  interface Props {
    user: User;
    variant?: "compact" | "full";
  }

  let { user, variant = "full" }: Props = $props();
</script>

<article class="user-card" class:compact={variant === "compact"}>
  <h3>{user.name}</h3>
  {#if variant === "full"}
    <p>{user.email}</p>
  {/if}
</article>

<style>
  .user-card {
    padding: var(--space-4);
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
    border-radius: var(--radius-md);
  }

  .compact {
    padding: var(--space-2);
  }
</style>
```

### Props with `$props()`

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  // Type-safe props with defaults
  interface Props {
    title: string;
    count?: number;
    variant?: "primary" | "secondary";
    onclick?: (event: MouseEvent) => void;
  }

  let { title, count = 0, variant = "primary", onclick }: Props = $props();
</script>
```

### Spreading Rest Props

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import type { HTMLButtonAttributes } from "svelte/elements";

  interface Props extends HTMLButtonAttributes {
    variant: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost";
    size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";
    loading?: boolean;
  }

  let { variant, size = "md", loading = false, children, ...rest }: Props = $props();
</script>

<button {...rest} disabled={rest.disabled || loading} class={variant}>
  {#if loading}
    <Spinner />
  {:else}
    {@render children()}
  {/if}
</button>
```

### Two-Way Binding with `$bindable()`

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  interface Props {
    value: string;
    label: string;
  }

  let { value = $bindable(), label }: Props = $props();

  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
</script>

<div>
  <label for={id}>{label}</label>
  <input {id} bind:value />
</div>
```

```svelte
<!-- Parent usage -->
<TextInput bind:value={query} label="Search" />
```

### Snippets (Replacing Slots)

Svelte 5 uses snippets instead of slots for content projection.

```svelte
<!-- Card.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import type { Snippet } from "svelte";

  interface Props {
    children: Snippet;
    header?: Snippet;
    footer?: Snippet;
  }

  let { children, header, footer }: Props = $props();
</script>

<section class="card">
  {#if header}
    <header class="card-header">
      {@render header()}
    </header>
  {/if}
  <div class="card-body">
    {@render children()}
  </div>
  {#if footer}
    <footer class="card-footer">
      {@render footer()}
    </footer>
  {/if}
</section>
```

```svelte
<!-- Usage -->
<Card>
  {#snippet header()}
    <h2>Settings</h2>
  {/snippet}

  <p>Main content goes here.</p>

  {#snippet footer()}
    <button onclick={save}>Save</button>
  {/snippet}
</Card>
```

### Typed Snippets with Parameters

```svelte
<!-- DataList.svelte -->
<script lang="ts" generics="T">
  import type { Snippet } from "svelte";

  interface Props {
    items: T[];
    keyExtractor: (item: T) => string;
    children: Snippet<[item: T, index: number]>;
    empty?: Snippet;
  }

  let { items, keyExtractor, children, empty }: Props = $props();
</script>

{#if items.length === 0}
  {#if empty}
    {@render empty()}
  {:else}
    <p>No items.</p>
  {/if}
{:else}
  <ul role="list">
    {#each items as item, index (keyExtractor(item))}
      <li>{@render children(item, index)}</li>
    {/each}
  </ul>
{/if}
```

```svelte
<!-- Usage -->
<DataList {items} keyExtractor={(u) => u.id}>
  {#snippet children(user)}
    <UserCard {user} />
  {/snippet}
  {#snippet empty()}
    <EmptyState title="No users" description="Invite your team." />
  {/snippet}
</DataList>
```

---

## Runes (Reactivity)

### `$state`

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  // Primitive state
  let count = $state(0);
  let query = $state("");
  let isOpen = $state(false);

  // Object state (deeply reactive)
  let form = $state({
    name: "",
    email: "",
    message: "",
  });

  // Array state
  let items = $state<Item[]>([]);
</script>
```

### `$derived`

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let items = $state<Item[]>([]);
  let query = $state("");

  // Derived — recomputes when dependencies change
  let filteredItems = $derived(items.filter((item) =>
    item.name.toLowerCase().includes(query.toLowerCase())
  ));

  let itemCount = $derived(filteredItems.length);

  // Complex derivation
  let sortedAndFiltered = $derived.by(() => {
    const filtered = items.filter((item) => item.active);
    return filtered.toSorted((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));
  });
</script>
```

### `$effect`

Use `$effect` only for synchronization with external systems — same philosophy as React's `useEffect`.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let query = $state("");

  // Sync with external system
  $effect(() => {
    document.title = `Search: ${query}`;
  });

  // Effect with cleanup
  $effect(() => {
    const controller = new AbortController();
    fetchResults(query, { signal: controller.signal });

    return () => controller.abort();
  });

  // Don't use $effect for derived state — use $derived instead
  // BAD:
  let filtered = $state<Item[]>([]);
  $effect(() => {
    filtered = items.filter((i) => i.active); // Don't do this
  });

  // GOOD:
  let filtered = $derived(items.filter((i) => i.active));
</script>
```

### `$effect.pre`

Runs before DOM update — for reading DOM state before it changes.

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let messages = $state<Message[]>([]);
  let container: HTMLDivElement;

  $effect.pre(() => {
    // Check scroll position before new messages render
    if (container) {
      const isAtBottom = container.scrollHeight - container.scrollTop === container.clientHeight;
      if (isAtBottom) {
        // After render, scroll to bottom
        $effect(() => {
          container.scrollTop = container.scrollHeight;
        });
      }
    }
  });
</script>
```

### `$inspect` (Development Only)

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let count = $state(0);

  // Logs whenever count changes — stripped in production
  $inspect(count);

  // With custom handler
  $inspect(count).with((type, value) => {
    if (type === "update") console.log("count changed to", value);
  });
</script>
```

---

## Error Handling

### Error Pages

```svelte
<!-- src/routes/+error.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import { page } from "$app/stores";
</script>

<div role="alert">
  <h1>
    {#if $page.status === 404}
      Page not found
    {:else}
      Something went wrong
    {/if}
  </h1>
  <p>{$page.error?.message}</p>
  <a href="/">Go home</a>
</div>
```

### `handleError` Hook

```ts
// src/hooks.server.ts
import type { HandleServerError } from "@sveltejs/kit";

export const handleError: HandleServerError = async ({ error, event, status, message }) => {
  console.error("Server error:", error);

  // Report to error tracking
  await reportError(error, { url: event.url.pathname, status });

  return {
    message: "An unexpected error occurred",
  };
};
```

### Component-Level Error Handling

```svelte
<!-- Use SvelteKit's built-in error boundary via +error.svelte at any route level -->
<!-- src/routes/dashboard/+error.svelte catches errors in /dashboard/* -->

<script lang="ts">
  import { page } from "$app/stores";
</script>

<div role="alert">
  <h2>Dashboard error</h2>
  <p>{$page.error?.message}</p>
  <a href="/dashboard">Reload dashboard</a>
</div>
```

---

## Data Loading

### Server Load Functions

```ts
// src/routes/users/+page.server.ts
import type { PageServerLoad } from "./$types";

export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ fetch, depends }) => {
  depends("app:users"); // For manual invalidation

  const response = await fetch("/api/users");
  if (!response.ok) {
    throw error(response.status, "Failed to load users");
  }

  const users: User[] = await response.json();
  return { users };
};
```

```svelte
<!-- src/routes/users/+page.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import type { PageData } from "./$types";

  let { data }: { data: PageData } = $props();
</script>

<main>
  <h1>Users</h1>
  {#if data.users.length === 0}
    <EmptyState title="No users" description="Invite your team." />
  {:else}
    <ul role="list">
      {#each data.users as user (user.id)}
        <li><UserCard {user} /></li>
      {/each}
    </ul>
  {/if}
</main>
```

### Parallel Loading

```ts
// src/routes/dashboard/+page.server.ts
import type { PageServerLoad } from "./$types";

export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ fetch }) => {
  // Parallel — not sequential
  const [metricsRes, activityRes, alertsRes] = await Promise.all([
    fetch("/api/metrics"),
    fetch("/api/activity"),
    fetch("/api/alerts"),
  ]);

  return {
    metrics: metricsRes.json(),
    activity: activityRes.json(),
    alerts: alertsRes.json(),
  };
};
```

### Streaming with Promises

```ts
// Return promises to stream data
export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ fetch }) => {
  // Critical data — awaited
  const user = await fetch("/api/user").then((r) => r.json());

  return {
    user,
    // Non-critical — streamed, resolves later
    metrics: fetch("/api/metrics").then((r) => r.json()),
    activity: fetch("/api/activity").then((r) => r.json()),
  };
};
```

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let { data } = $props();
</script>

<h1>Welcome, {data.user.name}</h1>

{#await data.metrics}
  <MetricsSkeleton />
{:then metrics}
  <DashboardMetrics {metrics} />
{:catch error}
  <ErrorCard message={error.message} />
{/await}
```

### Universal Load (Client + Server)

```ts
// src/routes/users/+page.ts (runs on both server and client)
import type { PageLoad } from "./$types";

export const load: PageLoad = async ({ fetch, url }) => {
  const page = url.searchParams.get("page") ?? "1";
  const users = await fetch(`/api/users?page=${page}`).then((r) => r.json());
  return { users, page: Number(page) };
};
```

---

## Form Actions

SvelteKit form actions provide progressive enhancement by default — forms work without JavaScript.

### Basic Form Action

```ts
// src/routes/contact/+page.server.ts
import type { Actions } from "./$types";
import { fail } from "@sveltejs/kit";
import { z } from "zod";

const ContactSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1, "Name is required"),
  email: z.string().email("Invalid email"),
  message: z.string().min(10, "Message must be at least 10 characters"),
});

export const actions: Actions = {
  default: async ({ request }) => {
    const formData = await request.formData();
    const parsed = ContactSchema.safeParse({
      name: formData.get("name"),
      email: formData.get("email"),
      message: formData.get("message"),
    });

    if (!parsed.success) {
      return fail(400, {
        errors: parsed.error.flatten().fieldErrors,
        values: Object.fromEntries(formData),
      });
    }

    await sendContactEmail(parsed.data);
    return { success: true };
  },
};
```

```svelte
<!-- src/routes/contact/+page.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import { enhance } from "$app/forms";
  import type { ActionData } from "./$types";

  let { form }: { form: ActionData } = $props();
</script>

<form method="POST" use:enhance>
  <div>
    <label for="name">Name</label>
    <input id="name" name="name" value={form?.values?.name ?? ""} required />
    {#if form?.errors?.name}
      <p role="alert" class="error">{form.errors.name[0]}</p>
    {/if}
  </div>

  <div>
    <label for="email">Email</label>
    <input id="email" name="email" type="email" value={form?.values?.email ?? ""} required />
    {#if form?.errors?.email}
      <p role="alert" class="error">{form.errors.email[0]}</p>
    {/if}
  </div>

  <div>
    <label for="message">Message</label>
    <textarea id="message" name="message" required>{form?.values?.message ?? ""}</textarea>
    {#if form?.errors?.message}
      <p role="alert" class="error">{form.errors.message[0]}</p>
    {/if}
  </div>

  <button type="submit">Send</button>

  {#if form?.success}
    <p role="status" class="success">Message sent!</p>
  {/if}
</form>
```

### Named Actions

```ts
// +page.server.ts
export const actions: Actions = {
  create: async ({ request }) => {
    // Handle create
  },
  delete: async ({ request }) => {
    // Handle delete
  },
};
```

```svelte
<!-- Specify action with formaction or action attribute -->
<form method="POST" action="?/create" use:enhance>
  <!-- create form -->
</form>

<form method="POST" action="?/delete" use:enhance>
  <input type="hidden" name="id" value={item.id} />
  <button type="submit">Delete</button>
</form>
```

### Custom `use:enhance`

```svelte
<form
  method="POST"
  use:enhance={() => {
    submitting = true;

    return async ({ result, update }) => {
      submitting = false;

      if (result.type === "success") {
        toast.success("Saved!");
      }

      await update(); // Apply default behavior (update form prop)
    };
  }}
>
```

---

## SvelteKit Conventions

### Route Structure

```
src/routes/
├── +layout.svelte           → Root layout
├── +page.svelte             → /
├── about/+page.svelte       → /about
├── users/
│   ├── +page.svelte         → /users
│   ├── +page.server.ts      → Server load + actions
│   ├── [id]/
│   │   ├── +page.svelte     → /users/:id
│   │   └── +page.server.ts
│   └── +error.svelte        → Error boundary for /users/*
├── api/
│   └── users/+server.ts     → API endpoint
└── +error.svelte            → Root error page
```

### Layouts

```svelte
<!-- src/routes/+layout.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import type { LayoutData } from "./$types";

  let { data, children }: { data: LayoutData; children: any } = $props();
</script>

<div class="layout">
  <nav aria-label="Main navigation">
    <MainNav user={data.user} />
  </nav>
  <main>
    {@render children()}
  </main>
  <footer>
    <AppFooter />
  </footer>
</div>
```

### Hooks (Middleware)

```ts
// src/hooks.server.ts
import type { Handle } from "@sveltejs/kit";
import { sequence } from "@sveltejs/kit/hooks";

const auth: Handle = async ({ event, resolve }) => {
  const session = event.cookies.get("session");

  if (session) {
    event.locals.user = await getUserFromSession(session);
  }

  if (!event.locals.user && event.url.pathname.startsWith("/dashboard")) {
    throw redirect(303, "/login");
  }

  return resolve(event);
};

const logging: Handle = async ({ event, resolve }) => {
  const start = Date.now();
  const response = await resolve(event);
  console.log(`${event.request.method} ${event.url.pathname} — ${Date.now() - start}ms`);
  return response;
};

export const handle = sequence(auth, logging);
```

### API Routes

```ts
// src/routes/api/users/+server.ts
import type { RequestHandler } from "./$types";
import { json, error } from "@sveltejs/kit";

export const GET: RequestHandler = async ({ url }) => {
  const page = Number(url.searchParams.get("page")) || 1;
  const users = await db.user.findMany({ skip: (page - 1) * 20, take: 20 });
  return json({ data: users, meta: { page } });
};

export const POST: RequestHandler = async ({ request }) => {
  const body = await request.json();
  const parsed = CreateUserSchema.safeParse(body);

  if (!parsed.success) {
    throw error(400, { message: "Validation failed" });
  }

  const user = await db.user.create({ data: parsed.data });
  return json({ data: user }, { status: 201 });
};
```

### SEO and Head

```svelte
<svelte:head>
  <title>Users — Dashboard</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Manage your team members." />
  <meta property="og:title" content="Users — Dashboard" />
  <meta property="og:description" content="Manage your team members." />
</svelte:head>
```

---

## State Management

### Decision Guide

| Scope | Solution |
|---|---|
| Local to one component | `$state` |
| Derived from state | `$derived` |
| Parent-child | `$props` + callbacks |
| Subtree injection | `setContext` / `getContext` |
| Global client state | Svelte stores or shared `$state` module |
| Server state | Load functions |
| URL state | `$page.url.searchParams` |

### Context

```svelte
<!-- ThemeProvider.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
  import { setContext } from "svelte";

  let theme = $state<"light" | "dark">("light");

  setContext("theme", {
    get theme() { return theme; },
    toggle() { theme = theme === "light" ? "dark" : "light"; },
  });
</script>

{@render children()}
```

```svelte
<!-- Consumer -->
<script lang="ts">
  import { getContext } from "svelte";

  const { theme, toggle } = getContext<{
    theme: "light" | "dark";
    toggle: () => void;
  }>("theme");
</script>

<button onclick={toggle}>
  Current: {theme}
</button>
```

### Shared State Module

```ts
// lib/state/auth.svelte.ts
let user = $state<User | null>(null);
let status = $state<"idle" | "loading" | "authenticated" | "error">("idle");

export const auth = {
  get user() { return user; },
  get status() { return status; },
  get isAuthenticated() { return status === "authenticated"; },

  async login(credentials: LoginCredentials) {
    status = "loading";
    try {
      user = await api.login(credentials);
      status = "authenticated";
    } catch {
      status = "error";
    }
  },

  logout() {
    user = null;
    status = "idle";
  },
};
```

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { auth } from "$lib/state/auth.svelte";
</script>

{#if auth.isAuthenticated}
  <p>Welcome, {auth.user?.name}</p>
  <button onclick={auth.logout}>Log out</button>
{/if}
```

---

## Performance

### Keyed `{#each}`

Always use a key for dynamic lists.

```svelte
{#each items as item (item.id)}
  <ItemCard {item} />
{/each}
```

### `$state.raw` for Large Immutable Data

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  // For large objects that don't need deep reactivity
  let largeDataset = $state.raw<DataPoint[]>([]);

  // Update by replacing the entire reference
  function loadData(data: DataPoint[]) {
    largeDataset = data;
  }
</script>
```

### Lazy Components

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  const HeavyChart = import("./HeavyChart.svelte");
</script>

{#await HeavyChart}
  <ChartSkeleton />
{:then module}
  <module.default data={chartData} />
{/await}
```

---

## Transitions and Animations

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { fade, slide, fly } from "svelte/transition";
  import { quintOut } from "svelte/easing";

  let isVisible = $state(true);
</script>

{#if isVisible}
  <div transition:fade={{ duration: 200 }}>
    Fades in and out
  </div>

  <div in:fly={{ y: 20, duration: 300 }} out:fade={{ duration: 200 }}>
    Flies in, fades out
  </div>
{/if}

<!-- List transitions -->
{#each items as item (item.id)}
  <div animate:flip={{ duration: 300 }} transition:slide>
    {item.name}
  </div>
{/each}
```

---

## Testing Patterns

Follow the project's existing testing setup. These patterns apply to Svelte with Vitest and @testing-library/svelte.

### Component Testing

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/svelte";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import UserCard from "./UserCard.svelte";

test("displays user name and email", () => {
  render(UserCard, {
    props: { user: { id: "1", name: "Jane Doe", email: "jane@example.com" } },
  });

  expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { name: "Jane Doe" })).toBeInTheDocument();
  expect(screen.getByText("jane@example.com")).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing User Interaction

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/svelte";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import Counter from "./Counter.svelte";

test("increments count on button click", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();
  render(Counter);

  expect(screen.getByText("Count: 0")).toBeInTheDocument();

  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "+1" }));

  expect(screen.getByText("Count: 1")).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing Bindable Props and Events

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/svelte";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";
import SearchInput from "./SearchInput.svelte";

test("fires search event when user types", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();
  const { component } = render(SearchInput);

  const events: string[] = [];
  component.$on("search", (e: CustomEvent<string>) => events.push(e.detail));

  await user.type(screen.getByRole("searchbox"), "hello");

  expect(events).toContain("hello");
});
```

### Testing Async Behavior

```ts
test("shows loading then data", async () => {
  render(UserProfile, { props: { userId: "123" } });

  expect(screen.getByText("Loading")).toBeInTheDocument();

  const heading = await screen.findByRole("heading", { name: "Jane Doe" });
  expect(heading).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

Rules:
- Use `@testing-library/svelte` for behavior-driven tests.
- Test rendered output, not rune values directly.
- Use MSW for API mocking (same as React/Vue).
- Group tests by behavior, not by component method.

---

## Common Anti-Patterns to Avoid

### Do Not

- **Use `$effect` for derived state.** Use `$derived` instead.
- **Mutate `$props` directly.** Props are read-only. Use callbacks or `$bindable`.
- **Use Svelte stores (`writable`, `readable`) in new Svelte 5 code.** Use runes instead. Stores are legacy.
- **Forget keys in `{#each}`.** Always provide a unique key: `{#each items as item (item.id)}`.
- **Use `onMount` for data loading.** Use SvelteKit load functions.
- **Skip `use:enhance` on forms.** Without it, forms cause full-page navigation.
- **Create `.svelte.ts` files without runes.** The `.svelte.ts` extension signals that the file uses runes.
- **Use `any` to silence TypeScript.** Fix the type instead.
- **Nest `$effect` inside `$effect`.** Effects should be flat. If you need dependent effects, restructure.
- **Use `$effect` for event handling.** Use event handlers directly.
- **Skip `use:enhance` on `<form method="POST">`.** Without it, forms cause full-page reloads.
- **Use `any` to silence TypeScript.** Fix the type instead.
- **Import heavy libraries in all pages.** Use dynamic imports or conditional loading.
- **Forget error pages.** Create `+error.svelte` at route boundaries.
- **Nest `{#each}` without keys.** Always use `{#each items as item (item.id)}`.

---

## When To Read References

Read only the files that match the current task:

- `references/semantic-html.md` — creating or restructuring markup
- `references/accessibility.md` — forms, dialogs, navigation, interactive UI
- `references/typescript-strict.md` — props, API data, state typing
- `references/ui-states.md` — loading, empty, error, success states
- `references/security.md` — user input, external content, links
- `references/responsive.md` — layouts, viewport-dependent UI
- `references/testing.md` — testing patterns (adapt React examples to Svelte Testing Library)
- `references/performance.md` — Core Web Vitals, bundle optimization
- `references/css-architecture.md` — styling decisions, theming, design tokens
- `references/review-checklist.md` — final review gate

Note: Some references contain React/Vue code examples. Translate patterns to Svelte idioms — the underlying principles are the same.

## Boundaries

This skill provides Svelte 5 and SvelteKit idioms.
For cross-framework standards (semantics, accessibility, typing, UX states), defer to `frontloom-core-fe`.
